items.headline: Torkelson's home run, McGonigle's RBI singles lift Tigers past Rangers, 7-1
items.description:
DETROIT (AP) — Spencer Torkelson hit a two-run homer and the Detroit Tigers pulled away to a 7-1 victory over the Texas Rangers on Sunday night.
Torkelson had gone five games without a homer after tying a franchise record by homering in five consecutive games.

items.headline: Canadiens outlast Lightning 2-1 in Game 7 to win thrilling 1st-round series
items.description:
TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — Alex Newhook broke a tie with 8:53 left and the Montreal Canadiens outlasted the Tampa Bay Lightning 2-1 on Game 7 on Sunday night to end the thrilling first-round series.
The Canadiens will face the Buffalo Sabres in the second round after finishing off their first series victory since losing the Stanley Cup Final to Tampa Bay in 2021. Game 1 is Wednesday night in Buffalo.

items.headline: Anthony Volpe's rehab ends and Yankees option shortstop to Triple-A with José Caballero playing well
items.description:
NEW YORK (AP) — Anthony Volpe’s rehab from left shoulder surgery ended Sunday, but the shortstop’s next step is not rejoining the New York Yankees.
With José Caballero excelling as New York's everyday shortstop, the AL East-leading Yankees announced they had optioned Volpe to Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre.

items.headline: Saka sparks Arsenal attack into life ahead of Atletico showdown
items.description: Bukayo Saka's return from injury has jolted Arsenal's attack back into life just in time for the Gunners to dream of a Premier...

items.headline: Atletico aim to show Alvarez their ambition in Arsenal semi
items.description: For Atletico Madrid striker Julian Alvarez, the consequences of Tuesday's Champions League semi-final clash at Arsenal may go beyond booking a ticket to Budapest...

items.headline: Paolo Banchero wonders about the Magic's future after their playoff collapse against the Pistons
items.description:
DETROIT (AP) — Paolo Banchero wanted to say the diplomatic thing.
He just couldn't do it.

items.headline: Royals hold Mariners to 4 hits for a 4-1 win and 3-game series sweep
items.description:
SEATTLE (AP) — Vinnie Pasquantino scored the go-ahead run in a three-run fourth inning and the Kansas City Royals held the Seattle Mariners to just four hits for a 4-1 win on Sunday.
Kris Bubic (3-1) pitched seven innings, allowing four hits, one earned run and striking out seven to finish off a three-game sweep.

items.headline: Raptors All-Star forward Brandon Ingram out for Game 7 against Cavaliers with sore right heel
items.description:
CLEVELAND (AP) — Toronto Raptors forward Brandon Ingram missed Game 7 of the Eastern Conference first-round playoff series against the Cleveland Cavaliers on Sunday because of a sore right heel.
"The injury right now is such that he needs to rest and go easy on his heel and we'll see where he is at after this," coach Darko Rajakovic said before the game.

items.headline: Chase Elliott wins at Texas to join Tyler Reddick as multiple Cup Series winners this season
items.description:
FORT WORTH, Texas (AP) — Chase Elliott went low, got a push from his Hendrick Motorsports teammate and held on to become only the second NASCAR Cup driver with multiple wins this season.
Elliott stayed ahead of Denny Hamlin for the closing four laps after a final restart at Texas on Sunday for his second victory this year. He finished just ahead of Hamlin in both of those wins.

items.headline: Cameron Young at Doral and Nelly Korda in Mexico deliver dominant wins
items.description:
DORAL, Fla. (AP) — Cameron Young called a one-shot penalty on himself for his ball slightly moving in the second fairway and he still made par. It was that kind of week for Young, who closed with a 4-under 68 for a six-shot victory over Scottie Scheffler in the Cadillac Championship.
Young became the first wire-to-wire winner at Doral since Andy Bean in 1977, opening with a 64 on the Blue Monster and never giving anyone much of a chance, including the world's No. 1 player.
